basic_details.job_title: WII Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 20 Project Assistant, Field Worker and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Wildlife Institute of India (WII), Chandrabani, Dehradun
basic_details.short_information: The Wildlife Institute of India (WII), Dehradun, an autonomous institution under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), Government of India, has released the WII Recruitment 2026 notification (Advt. No. WII/ADVT. 1/RP–CELL/July, 2026) inviting online applications from Indian nationals for 20 project positions across five ongoing research projects. This article covers every detail from the official notification, including important dates, vacancy breakup, eligibility, salary, application fee, selection process, and the step-by-step online application procedure.

important_dates.raw: Online Application Start Date: 14 July 2026 Online Application Last Date: 28 July 2026 | No printed or offline application copies will be accepted. Applicants must apply only through the official WII-RP Cell portal.

application_fee.details: Payment can be made via online banking, NEFT/RTGS, UPI, or offline mode (cash/demand draft) in favor of "RRP Cell Revolving Fund/Director Wildlife Institute of India", Union Bank of India, WII Branch, Chandrabani, Dehradun. Account No.: 518502010059776, IFSC: UBIN0551856. The payment receipt must be uploaded with the application. The fee is non-refundable, even if the application is rejected or withdrawn.

age_limit.details: Position Category Upper Age Limit Administrative Assistant / Project Assistant / Senior Project Associate / Project Associate-II 50 years Project Associate-I 35 years | Age relaxation applicable as per Government of India norms: | SC/ST candidates: up to 5 years OBC candidates: up to 3 years

qualification.details: Eligibility varies by position and project. Below is a category-wise breakdown as per the official notification. | Administrative Assistant / Scientific Administrative Assistant / Field Worker: Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognized institute/University. Project Assistant (Science-based projects): Bachelor's Degree in Science (Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Marine Sciences, Marine Biology, Environmental Sciences, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences, Agricultural or Forestry Sciences). Project Assistant (Social Science): Bachelor's Degree in any Social Science discipline (Geography, Statistics, Economics, Public Administration, Personnel Management, Environmental Management, Social Work, Anthropology). Project Associate-I: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree (as per NEP) or Master's Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Marine Sciences/Biology, Environmental Sciences, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences)/Agricultural/Forestry Sciences, OR Bachelor's degree in Veterinary Science. Project Associate-II: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree or Master's Degree in Natural Sciences/Agricultural Sciences with 2 years' relevant R&D experience, OR BVSc with 2 years' experience, OR Bachelor's in Engineering/Technology (CS/IT/Remote Sensing/GIS/Data/Electrical/Electronics/AI) with 2 years' experience. Senior Project Associate: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree with 4 years' R&D experience, OR Master's Degree with 4 years' R&D experience, OR a Doctoral Degree in Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Life Sciences. | Desirable qualifications (such as prior field experience, GIS software knowledge, publications, or regional preference for candidates from Andaman & Nicobar Islands) differ by project. Refer to the official notification for the exact desirable criteria of each post. | Age relaxation applicable as per Government of India norms: | SC/ST candidates: up to 5 years OBC candidates: up to 3 years

vacancy_details.Project: Conservation and Long-term Monitoring of Sea Turtles, Andaman & Nicobar Islands
Category 2 Centre on World Natural Heritage Management
Environmental Flow Assessment for Chambal and Tons Rivers
Impact Assessment of Vibrations on Aquatic Life due to Underwater Tunnel Construction (River Brahmaputra)

post_wise_breakdown.project: Conservation and Long-term Monitoring of Sea Turtles, Andaman & Nicobar Islands
Category 2 Centre on World Natural Heritage Management
Environmental Flow Assessment for Chambal and Tons Rivers
Impact Assessment of Vibrations on Aquatic Life due to Underwater Tunnel Construction (River Brahmaputra)

salary_details.details: Administrative Assistant / Scientific Administrative Assistant / Field Worker: ₹24,000 + HRA Project Assistant: ₹27,000 + HRA Project Associate-I: ₹37,000 + HRA (NET/GATE qualified) or ₹30,000 + HRA (Non-NET/GATE) Project Associate-II: ₹42,000 + HRA (NET/GATE qualified) or ₹33,000 + HRA (Non-NET/GATE) Senior Project Associate: ₹57,000 + HRA

selection_process: Shortlisting for the online interview is based on essential qualification
and publications. The scoring pattern differs by post category:
% Marks in Bachelor's Degree: 25 Marks Specialization relevance to the project (via internship
% Marks in Master's Degree: 25 Marks Specialization relevance to the project: 25 Marks
UGC/CSIR/ICAR NET with JRF or WII-NET Passed: 5 Marks % Marks in Post-Graduation: 25 Marks Research/Work Experience: 10 Marks Publications (journals

The top 10 shortlisted candidates for each advertised project/position will be called for an online interview. Meeting the minimum qualification does not guarantee an interview call. The Institute's decision on eligibility
and selection is final and binding.
how_to_apply: Visit the official WII-RP Cell application portal: https://wiirp.com/application.php, or scan the QR code given in the official notification.

Keep scanned copies of all required documents (educational certificates, marksheets, experience letters, category certificate, NET/GATE certificate, publications, photograph, signature, and fee receipt) ready before starting.
Register and fill in the online application form carefully, entering educational details exactly as per your marksheets.
Pay the application/processing fee online (or via NEFT/RTGS/offline mode) and upload the payment receipt in the application form.
Upload all supporting documents as specified.
Review and submit the application before the last date, 28 July 2026.
If applying for more than one position, submit a separate online application form with fee and documents for each position (maximum of two positions relevant to your academic background).
important_instructions: Only online applications are accepted; no printed application copies will be considered. A candidate may apply for a maximum of two advertised positions relevant to their academic background and eligibility. Separate application forms, documents, and fees are required for each position applied to. Candidates currently engaged with a WII project or pursuing a Ph.D. must submit a recent No Objection Certificate. All positions are purely temporary and linked to project duration; they stand abolished on project completion. Canvassing in any form will lead to rejection of candidature. The list of shortlisted candidates and interview results will be published under the "Latest News & Announcements" section of the WII website. Selected candidates must join the project position immediately within the duration mentioned in the offer letter. The Director, WII, reserves the right to reject any application without assigning a reason.

rows.details: Wildlife Institute of India (WII), Chandrabani, Dehradun
20 posts across 5 research projects (Administrative Assistant, Project Assistant, Project Associate-I/II, Senior Project Associate, Scientific Administrative Assistant/Field Worker)
Purely temporary, project-based (tenure tied to project duration)

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Online Application Start Date: 14 July 2026 Online Application Last Date: 28 July 2026 No printed or offline application copies will be accepted. Applicants must apply only through the official WII-RP Cell portal.

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The recruitment covers 20 posts spread across five projects. The table below summarizes each position, number of vacancies, monthly emoluments, and age limit as per the official notification. Total Vacancies: 20

rows.project: Conservation and Long-term Monitoring of Sea Turtles, Andaman & Nicobar Islands
Category 2 Centre on World Natural Heritage Management
Environmental Flow Assessment for Chambal and Tons Rivers
Impact Assessment of Vibrations on Aquatic Life due to Underwater Tunnel Construction (River Brahmaputra)

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility varies by position and project. Below is a category-wise breakdown as per the official notification. Administrative Assistant / Scientific Administrative Assistant / Field Worker: Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognized institute/University. Project Assistant (Science-based projects): Bachelor's Degree in Science (Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Marine Sciences, Marine Biology, Environmental Sciences, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences, Agricultural or Forestry Sciences). Project Assistant (Social Science): Bachelor's Degree in any Social Science discipline (Geography, Statistics, Economics, Public Administration, Personnel Management, Environmental Management, Social Work, Anthropology). Project Associate-I: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree (as per NEP) or Master's Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science, Zoology, Ecology, Marine Sciences/Biology, Environmental Sciences, Geoinformatics, Life Sciences)/Agricultural/Forestry Sciences, OR Bachelor's degree in Veterinary Science. Project Associate-II: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree or Master's Degree in Natural Sciences/Agricultural Sciences with 2 years' relevant R&D experience, OR BVSc with 2 years' experience, OR Bachelor's in Engineering/Technology (CS/IT/Remote Sensing/GIS/Data/Electrical/Electronics/AI) with 2 years' experience. Senior Project Associate: Four-Year Bachelor's Degree with 4 years' R&D experience, OR Master's Degree with 4 years' R&D experience, OR a Doctoral Degree in Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Life Sciences. Desirable qualifications (such as prior field experience, GIS software knowledge, publications, or regional preference for candidates from Andaman & Nicobar Islands) differ by project. Refer to the official notification for the exact desirable criteria of each post. Age relaxation applicable as per Government of India norms: SC/ST candidates: up to 5 years OBC candidates: up to 3 years

rows.position_category: Administrative Assistant / Project Assistant / Senior Project Associate / Project Associate-II

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Salary (Monthly Emoluments)
content_sections.text: Administrative Assistant / Scientific Administrative Assistant / Field Worker: ₹24,000 + HRA Project Assistant: ₹27,000 + HRA Project Associate-I: ₹37,000 + HRA (NET/GATE qualified) or ₹30,000 + HRA (Non-NET/GATE) Project Associate-II: ₹42,000 + HRA (NET/GATE qualified) or ₹33,000 + HRA (Non-NET/GATE) Senior Project Associate: ₹57,000 + HRA

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Payment can be made via online banking, NEFT/RTGS, UPI, or offline mode (cash/demand draft) in favor of "RRP Cell Revolving Fund/Director Wildlife Institute of India", Union Bank of India, WII Branch, Chandrabani, Dehradun. Account No.: 518502010059776, IFSC: UBIN0551856. The payment receipt must be uploaded with the application. The fee is non-refundable, even if the application is rejected or withdrawn.

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Shortlisting for the online interview is based on essential qualification, age limit, percentage of marks, specialization relevance, research experience, and publications. The scoring pattern differs by post category: % Marks in Bachelor's Degree: 25 Marks Specialization relevance to the project (via internship, volunteering, or work experience): 25 Marks % Marks in Master's Degree: 25 Marks Specialization relevance to the project: 25 Marks UGC/CSIR/ICAR NET with JRF or WII-NET Passed: 5 Marks % Marks in Post-Graduation: 25 Marks Research/Work Experience: 10 Marks Publications (journals, conferences, book chapters): 10 Marks The top 10 shortlisted candidates for each advertised project/position will be called for an online interview. Meeting the minimum qualification does not guarantee an interview call. The Institute's decision on eligibility, shortlisting, and selection is final and binding.

content_sections.heading: WII Recruitment 2026: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Proof of age Educational qualification certificates and marksheets Work experience certificates mentioning start and end tenure, issued by employer/guide/supervisor Reserved category certificate (if applicable) UGC/CSIR/NET qualification certificate (if applicable) Proof of publications (if applicable) Passport-size photograph and signature Application Processing Fee payment receipt No Objection Certificate (for candidates currently working with any WII project or Ph.D. scholars, not older than one month from the last date of application) No Dues Certificate (for candidates who previously worked at WII) Applications without these supporting documents are liable to be rejected.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for WII Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official WII-RP Cell application portal: https://wiirp.com/application.php , or scan the QR code given in the official notification. Keep scanned copies of all required documents (educational certificates, marksheets, experience letters, category certificate, NET/GATE certificate, publications, photograph, signature, and fee receipt) ready before starting. Register and fill in the online application form carefully, entering educational details exactly as per your marksheets. Pay the application/processing fee online (or via NEFT/RTGS/offline mode) and upload the payment receipt in the application form. Upload all supporting documents as specified. Review and submit the application before the last date, 28 July 2026. If applying for more than one position, submit a separate online application form with fee and documents for each position (maximum of two positions relevant to your academic background).

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Only online applications are accepted; no printed application copies will be considered. A candidate may apply for a maximum of two advertised positions relevant to their academic background and eligibility. Separate application forms, documents, and fees are required for each position applied to. Candidates currently engaged with a WII project or pursuing a Ph.D. must submit a recent No Objection Certificate. All positions are purely temporary and linked to project duration; they stand abolished on project completion. Canvassing in any form will lead to rejection of candidature. The list of shortlisted candidates and interview results will be published under the "Latest News & Announcements" section of the WII website. Selected candidates must join the project position immediately within the duration mentioned in the offer letter. The Director, WII, reserves the right to reject any application without assigning a reason.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for WII Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Wildlife Institute of India (WII) has announced a total of 20 vacancies for various Research Project Positions. This recruitment drive offers opportunities across multiple scientific and administrative roles within the institute.

faq.question: Q2. What are the application start and end dates for WII Research Project Positions?
faq.answer: The online application process for WII Research Project Positions starts on 14 July 2026. The last date to submit applications online is 28 July 2026, so candidates should apply within this period.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for WII Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application mode for the WII Research Project Positions Recruitment 2026 is online. Interested and eligible candidates must submit their applications through the official website of the Wildlife Institute of India.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age relaxation for reserved categories in WII Recruitment?
faq.answer: For the WII Research Project Positions, age relaxation is provided for reserved categories. SC/ST candidates can avail up to 5 years of relaxation, while OBC candidates are eligible for up to 3 years of age relaxation.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for these Research Project Positions at WII?
faq.answer: The selection process for the WII Research Project Positions involves two main stages: initial shortlisting based on marks and experience, followed by an online interview for the shortlisted candidates. Performance in the interview will be crucial.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for General/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: General and OBC category candidates applying for the WII Research Project Positions are required to pay an application fee of Rs. 500/-. This fee must be paid online during the application submission process.
